name: [BUG-005] Product creation allows price=0 without warning
description:
Validation gap: POST /api/products/ accepts price=0, creating a
product that is effectively free. While a zero-price product may be
intentional in some business models, no warning or explicit flag is
required, making it trivially easy to accidentally set a price to 0.
A minimum price validation (price > 0) or an explicit 'is_free'
boolean flag would prevent accidental free listings.

Expected: 400 Bad Request or at minimum a validation warning for price=0.
Actual:   201 Created — product with price=$0.00 is accepted.
descriptionHtml:
Validation gap: POST /api/products/ accepts price=0, creating a
product that is effectively free. While a zero-price product may be
intentional in some business models, no warning or explicit flag is
required, making it trivially easy to accidentally set a price to 0.
A minimum price validation (price > 0) or an explicit 'is_free'
boolean flag would prevent accidental free listings.

Expected: 400 Bad Request or at minimum a validation warning for price=0.
Actual: 201 Created — product with price=$0.00 is accepted.
statusMessage:
XFAIL BUG-005: POST /api/products/ allows price=0 — free products can be created unintentionally

AssertionError: Expected 400 for price=0 but got 201. Products with zero price should be explicitly flagged or rejected.
assert 201 == 400
 +  where 201 = <WrapperTestResponse 299 bytes [201 CREATED]>.status_code
statusTrace:
tests\api\test_known_failures.py:151: in test_create_product_zero_price_not_rejected
    assert res.status_code == 400, (
E   AssertionError: Expected 400 for price=0 but got 201. Products with zero price should be explicitly flagged or rejected.
E   assert 201 == 400
E    +  where 201 = <WrapperTestResponse 299 bytes [201 CREATED]>.status_code
